posts.caption:
@charli_xcx cleared her entire IG feed, leaving only a single black-and-white photo of herself wearing headphones. And she wrote a simple caption: “i love making things.” Is this kind of resolution that began alongside her new album preparation? Or perhaps a declaration of a new narrative that only begins once everything has been stripped away? The more you remove what’s unnecessary, the clearer certain things become. Sometimes, the act of subtraction itself becomes the strongest form of art.
Charli xcx’s new IG account is @b.sides. It features black-and-white photos capturing her songwriting process, and everyday moments with friends. Perhaps what was revealed before the albums is her life itself. Is she preparing a new album? Or is the entire process already part of the album itself? Instead of excessive direction, natural photos that carry emotion leave behind a sense of authenticity. What this era needs is authenticity. A little closer, a little more raw. That’s why it makes me more curious. What the next content from @b.sides will be, and the next album that might be quietly being created right beside it. Swipe to see the changed mood of Charli xcx! Either way, Charli is always amazing.
Next-Level Fashion. Daily.
Follow @a2z_fashionmag Now.
📷 @charli_xcx, @b.sides
#Charlixcx #A2Zmusic

posts.caption:
Have you heard that Madonna’s new album, ‘Confessions II’, is set to be released on 3 July 2026? Today, the editor rewatched the music video for “Hung Up,” a track from her album ‘Confessions on a Dance Floor’. That energy—truly, the kind of energy one may have forgotten over time—was felt once again. It is hard to believe she was already 47 years old when she released the song. The music video was even shot in London. While waiting for Madonna’s new album, swipe to revisit the “Hung Up” music video.

posts.caption:
@indyamoore’s performance in Father Mother Sister Brother shimmers with warm authenticity. ⁠
⁠
Jim Jarmusch’s 14th feature is a tight triptych of unconnected stories that come together to say something profound about family ties. In the third vignette, Moore – a former AnOther Magazine cover star – and Luka Sabbat play a sister and brother who return to their childhood home in Paris after the death of their parents. Though their predicament is sadder than the film’s other characters, theirs is the most hopeful story. Moore’s Skye and Sabbat’s Billy no longer live in one another’s pockets, but their sibling bond has endured. ⁠
⁠
Moore, who broke through in 2018 by playing resilient sex worker Angel Vasquez-Evangelista in the groundbreaking LGBTQ+ series Pose, channelled their own grief into Skye’s. While they were filming Father Mother Sister Brother in early 2024, they were also using their profile to draw attention to the plight of genocide survivors in Palestine. “I was interfacing every single day with people who were trying to evacuate Gaza,” Moore says. “I was seeing images of people’s wounds, of children who were severely hurt, and I had to reckon with the weight of that on my body.” ⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, they speak candidly to @misternicklevine about acting, biotechnology, and serving as an advocate for causes including Palestine, the dismantling of white supremacy, and LGBTQ+ rights 📲⁠
⁠
📸 Father Mother Sister Brother, 2026. Photography by Yorick Le Saux⁠
⁠
#IndyaMoore
posts.alt_text: Photo by AnOther Magazine on April 15, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, people standing, magazine and text that says 'AnOther Indya Moore on Grief, Family Ties and Activism'.

posts.caption:
In New Genesis, his second book following Rotting from Within, photographer @kircherabdul continues his intimate exploration of human experience, this time documenting the life of Sierra Kiss, a single mother navigating a precarious existence in Los Angeles. ⁠
⁠
Published by @jointsloose and consisting of 144 pages, the photo book exposes the frailties of the systems meant to support vulnerable populations – shelters, churches, and social services, all eroded by chronic underfunding and restrictive policies, leaving individuals like Sierra to fend for themselves. Through Kircher’s lens, these structural failures are not abstract statistics – they are lived realities, marked by personal, intimate moments of crisis, fleeting relief, and Sierra’s daily life.⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, Kircher speaks with @karmmaacoma about capturing the daily rhythm of Sierra Kiss's life 📲⁠
⁠
📸 New Genesis © @kircherabdul, 2026. Courtesy of @jointsloose
posts.alt_text: Photo by AnOther Magazine on April 13, 2026. May be an image of child, magazine, poster and text that says 'AnOther Abdulhamid Kircher's Unflinching Portrait of a Single Mother in Los Angeles'.

posts.caption:
There’s a particular kind of designer who insists their work is not about them. Then there’s womenswear designer @fidannovruzovastudio, who is refreshingly honest about the opposite. “I design by projecting my own needs,” she smiles.⁠
⁠
The key reference point of her Autumn/Winter 2026 collection is Art Deco portrait artist Tamara de Lempicka, and the idea that she painted her muses as versions of herself. “They look like themselves, but they also look like her,” Novruzova explains. “I quite resonated with the idea of an auto-portrait of a muse.” The campaign, launching exclusively with AnOther, pushes that idea further.⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, the designer speaks with @georgepistachio about making clothes with an emphasis on how they function in real life. Read more at the link in bio 📲⁠
⁠
Photography by @philengelhardt ⁠

posts.caption:
In 2010, Sheffield-born @johnymodern travelled around Europe to search for the meaning of ‘Afropean’. “When I first heard Afropean, it encouraged me to think of myself as whole and unhyphenated,” he explains at the beginning of his critically-acclaimed book Afropean: Notes from Black Europe, published in 2019. ⁠
⁠
Coined in the 1990s, the term was first used in the realms of music and fashion before Pitts reframed it as a utopian alternative – a more positive label for Europe’s Black communities. Beneath the semantics is a desire for inclusion and belonging, with the question at the heart of the project being: Is there a collective, Black, European identity? ⁠
⁠
Since its publication, Afropean has expanded and evolved into photo books, podcasts, album covers – including a recent collaboration with Blood Orange’s Dev Hynes – exhibitions, and other writings. The latest iteration is Black Bricolage at @mep.paris in Paris, an exhibition that fittingly returns Pitts’ empathetic and discerning eye to France. Though he travelled across Europe – from Scandinavia to Russia – a large section of the book focuses on France, a melting pot of ethnicities.⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, the photographer speaks with @lydunishka about the new exhibition 📲⁠
⁠
📸 Black Bricolage by Johny Pitts. © Johny Pitts

posts.caption:
Between walking runways and fronting campaigns internationally to her silver screen debut in ‘Anyone but You’, @charleefraser is a multi-talented woman. But among Fraser’s manifold skills is one talent that she has, until now, kept on the quiet. “I love roller skating,” she shares. “I’m constantly thinking with the work that I do and [my] schedule. I’m a major organiser – such a Capricorn,” she laughs. “[With] my skates on, the only thing I can think about is what I’m doing. And it just really grounds me, brings me back into that present.”
Through the link in bio, see Charlee Fraser photographed at sunrise on the Northern Beaches of Sydney for BAZAAR.
Charlee Fraser wears @ralphlauren Collection in the April 2026 issue of Harper’s BAZAAR Australia/New Zealand.

posts.caption:
“Oh, man, little baby Charlee,” @charleefraser chuckles as she reflects on her career beginnings. “It was really interesting because I wasn’t looking to model at the time – the opportunity kind of presented itself,” she says of her beginning. By 2016, Fraser had been modelling locally for three years, with early gigs including runway appearances for David Jones, Carla Zampatti and Bec + Bridge. “My agency was like, ‘I think it’s time for you to go overseas. You’ve done everything here. Go spread the wings’,” she says. “At the time, I really felt ready for that.”
Continue reading Charlee Fraser’s interview with BAZAAR at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
When Elliot Hill returned to Nike as chief executive in October 2024, he was tasked with reversing one of the most significant slumps in the company’s history.
The business had lost momentum with both investors and consumers and his strategy has focused on restoring wholesale relationships, rebuilding key categories like running and trying to stabilise the brand’s broader narrative.
But Nike’s latest earnings and weak outlook have intensified doubts about whether the recovery is moving quickly enough. In a fragmented marketplace where heat has moved toward niche competitors and rejuvenated legacy rivals, Nike is struggling to convince a skeptical public and an impatient Wall Street that its next chapter has truly begun.
On this week’s episode of The Debrief, Sykes (@mikedsykes) joins hosts Sheena Butler-Young (@sheena.butler.young) and Brian Baskin (@bbaskin512) to unpack why Nike’s comeback still feels unfinished, what the brand is getting right, and what it would take for the market to believe again.
🎙️ Listen now #linkinbio
📷 Nike

posts.caption:
Uganda is ripe for investment but is often overshadowed by its larger neighbour, retail leaders in Kampala say. While Kenya’s status as East Africa’s shopping hub is not disputed, they add that Uganda has the potential to narrow the gap, especially in the mass market.
Apart from footwear-focused brands like Adidas, Puma and Skechers, few Western retailers have made the leap to Uganda. This has led locals to warn that while American and European brands hesitate, competitors from Asia and the Middle East are gaining first-mover advantage in a high-growth market.
Kingsley Kobo explores the shifting power dynamics in Uganda’s fashion market. Read more #linkinbio
📷 @abryanzstyleandfashionawards, @acacia_mall_ug, @opticauganda
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 15, 2026. May be an image of gown, magazine, dress, headdress and text that says 'BOF Why Uganda Is East Africa's Next Fashion Frontier'.

posts.caption:
Dolce & Gabbana has long defended its independence by borrowing.
Rather than selling equity or joining a larger group, the Milan-based fashion house relied on debt to finance expansion into capital-intensive businesses such as beauty and hospitality, preserving founder control while building scale.
That strategy is now colliding with a market that’s turned harsher.
BoF's Eric Sylvers (@ericsylvers) explores how the Italian luxury house is managing the mounting cost of its independence. Read more #linkinbio
📷 Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 15, 2026. May be an image of standing, magazine and text that says 'BOF Can Dolce & Gabbana Stay Independent?'.

posts.caption:
For decades now, Ralph Lauren has unquestionably been the brand at the top of the fashion pyramid in tennis. The Australian Open’s recent move to name Hugo Boss its lifestyle partner came as a surprise to fans of both tennis and the American brand, which is omnipresent at most of the sport's elite tournaments.
In a statement, Ralph Lauren affirmed its commitment to tennis, noting its long-term relationships with Winbledon, the US Open and the US Olympic teams. “While the 2026 tournament marks the final year of our sponsorship, we remain deeply committed to sports as an inherent part of our brand’s identity and a powerful way we connect with consumers across generations globally," the brand said."
In BoF’s latest The Kicks You Wear newsletter, Mike Sykes (@mikedsykes) explores what the Australian Open's sponsorship switch means for Ralph Lauren’s position as the standard bearer for fashion in tennis for decades. Read more and subscribe #linkinbio
📷 @ralphlauren
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 15, 2026. May be an image of tennis, racket, sportswear, sports equipment and text that says 'BOF THE KICKS YOU WEAR Ralph Lauren's S Grip Loosens on Tennis BY MIKE SYKES'.

posts.caption:
For #BazaarIndia’s 17th anniversary issue, actor Ananya Panday (@AnanyaPanday) shares that she loves being alone now. “I used to be someone who couldn’t be alone at all. I’d be scared to sit alone for even 10 minutes. I always needed someone around me — my partner, my parents, my friends,” admits Panday. “But when I started living alone, even though I’m in the same building as my parents, that little bit of space changed everything. Building your own environment and doing things your own way has shifted my love for space.”
Much like our cover installation, which conveys the negotiation of the fragile space between two people, the artwork by How Are You Feeling Studio features two film cranes facing each other like figures. Their machine bodies, covered in soft fabric, suggest an unexpected intimacy.
Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

Harper’s Bazaar India’s March cover star Ananya Panday (@AnanyaPanday) is unafraid of being vulnerable. She’s all too aware of the impermanence of fame and of not taking things for granted. “I definitely still have some sense of imposter syndrome. If I hear my name out loud, like someone saying ‘Ananya Panday’ or introducing me somewhere, for a second I kind of disassociate and I can’t fully respond to it,” she admits.

posts.caption:
This spring, it's all about tradwife with a twist. For a recording of "Live with Kelly and Mark" in New York on Wednesday, @ellefanning wore a girlish iridescent tutu and a buttery yellow polo shirt. From the waist down, she was dressed in her shimmering Sunday best.
From the waist up, the look was slouchy and boy-ish — like an off-duty tennis player, or someone's golf-loving father. The worlds-colliding look was designed by Paris-based brand August Barron, helmed by August Vestbø and Benjamin Barron, who subvert feminine and housewife aesthetics through unexpected layering, silhouettes and cutouts. Or often just by adding big black leather boots.
Fanning is currently promoting the Apple TV show "Margo's Got Money Troubles," and so far her outfits for the press tour have largely followed this line: slouchy, nostalgic and at times ironic versions of femininity. For her "Today" show appearance, Fanning wore the opening look from @rabanne Fall-Winter 2026 collection, which was inspired by tea dresses from the 1940s. The pinafore-style dress was in a twee ditsy print and flowed well past her knees, while a leather belt sat happily on her waist without exerting effort to cinch. On Monday, Fanning slipped into an equally vintage-inspired @albertaferretti rose-colored satin dress, covered in even more florals, with a built-in neckerchief.
📸: XNY/Star Max/GC Images/Getty Images; Cindy Ord/Getty Images; Jose Perez/Bauer-Griffin/GC Images/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 09, 2026 tagging @ellefanning. May be an image of one or more people, people standing, newspaper, magazine, poster and text that says 'ELLE FANNING MAKES THE CASE FOR SLOUCHY, OLD-SCHOOL FEMININITY CNN'.

posts.caption:
A piece of the Parisian landmark's original staircase is going up for auction in the French capital next month.
There are a couple of catches, however: Bidders will need deep pockets to buy it and high ceilings to store it.
Section no. 1 of the @toureiffelofficielle's original spiral staircase connected the second and third floors of the tower when it opened in 1889. It comprises of 14 steps and measures 9ft high.
According to a listing on @artcurial__'s website, it is expected to fetch between $140,000 and $175,000.
"It has never been outside and underwent a complete restoration for the sale," Sabrina Dolla, associate director at Artcurial, told CNN. "It's an immersive experience, a stationary journey through time and space. Imagine yourself in 1889 on this staircase, perched between 113 and 276 meters high, with no safety barriers, but with a 360-degree view of Paris."
📸 : saiko3p/iStockphoto/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 09, 2026 tagging @toureiffelofficielle, and @artcurial__. May be an image of Sacré-Cœur, tower and text that says 'A PIECE OF THE EIFFEL TOWER IS BEING AUCTIONED - YOU'LL NEED DEEP POCKETS CNN'.

posts.caption:
Was there a dress code at the @euphoria season 3 premiere?
Across the desert-style red carpet we saw corseted hot pants, sports-mode sunglasses, vintage gowns, T-shirts, tube tops, sequinned mini-dresses and metallic bustiers. It was a fashion hodge podge that skewed more toward what one might wear to a festival — which made sense, given HBO Max is set to screen the first episode of the new season at @coachella on Sunday.
Even the main cast were on different sartorial wavelengths. @alexademie made her red carpet return in an ultra glam vintage '90s @muglerofficial gown, @zendaya was snapped in an slinky @ashistudio dress and @sydney_sweeney arrived in a white a '60s-style archival cape mini dress from @pierrecardin. @hunterschafer wore a sexy @roberto_cavalli animal print look, while @maudeapatow was in prim-and-proper, hot-off-the-runway @celine. In many ways, it was a welcome gear-shift in an age of hyper-curated, method dressing red carpets.
📸 : Jordan Strauss/Presley Ann/Monica Schipper/Mario Anzuoni/Invision/AP/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 08, 2026 tagging @zendaya, @ashistudio, @euphoria, and @hbomax. May be an image of one or more people, gown, dress and text that says 'max CNN'.

posts.caption:
If Taylor Swift's record-breaking engagement announcement is any indication, Polymarket bets, group chats and neighborhood wagers are undoubtedly sprouting up around the world to guess what she might wear for her upcoming wedding to Travis Kelce.
But Kelce is no fashion slouch himself. A former GQ cover star, the Kansas City Chiefs player has helped spread the tunnel fit phenomenon with his vintage-inflected suits and goofy statement print pieces — helping to rebrand the logistical walkway into a surprising fashion moment for sporting stars. Kelce's tailoring choices on the day Swift walks down the aisle will certainly make news, too.
This week, a clue appeared in the form of a new partnership between Kelce and @tommyhilfiger. It suggests that, just as Swift wore American fashion hero @ralphlauren for her viral engagement photos on Instagram, Kelce may integrate a patriotic great into his matrimonial trousseau. On Monday, Tommy Hilfiger announced that Kelce will join the label as both a global brand ambassador and a creative collaborator.
Tap the link in bio for more.
📸 : Christopher Polk/Billboard/Getty Images, Courtesy of Tommy Hilfiger

posts.caption:
Karl Lagerfeld, who was known to have a portfolio of lavish homes, once said: "The most beautiful house is always the next one."
Your next house could be this graceful French mansion, previously owned by both the late German designer and by European royalty.
The seven-bedroom property, situated less than an hour from Paris, is on sale for 2.7 million euros ($3.1 million).
According to the property listing with realtor PYLA Paris, Manoir du Mée "is an elegant 18th-century residence" that previously belonged to Lagerfeld and then to Princess Caroline of Monaco.
Lagerfeld, who died in Paris in 2019, owned the property for at least a decade until 1998. @chanelofficial staged photo shoots there during that time, including one featuring French supermodel @inesdelafressangeofficial.
Princess Caroline and her family later lived there for six years, during which time her children attended a local school. Her daughter, Charlotte, drew on the walls upstairs and some of that artwork still remains, according to Alexis Feyfant, owner of real estate agency PYLA Paris.
The house was built in 1749 for a notable local family, the Faguiers, Feyfant told CNN. He said another distinguished resident was Renée Saint-Cyr, a French film actress who made her name in the 1930s.
📸 : Matteo Merea/PylaParis.fr

posts.caption:
In the 1960s, Pierre Cardin emerged as a pioneering figure of fashion futurism, redefining the visual language of an entire generation. His work introduced a radical break from traditional tailoring, replacing it with geometric forms, synthetic materials, and space-age silhouettes that reflected the era’s fascination with technology and progress.
Through sharp lines, experimental fabrics such as vinyl and plexiglass, and bold, graphic compositions, he constructed a new aesthetic that was less about the body and more about form and concept. His designs-ranging from sculptural dresses to androgynous, streamlined menswear, aligned with a broader cultural shift toward modernity and youth-driven identity. ￼
Rather than following fashion, Cardin projected it forward. His vision did not simply reflect the 1960s—it actively shaped its perception of the future, establishing a visual code that would influence designers and define the decade’s cultural imagination.
posts.alt_text: Photo by CULT MAGAZINEⓇ on April 15, 2026. May be an image of standing, costume, turtleneck, headscarf and text that says 'His Style defined α whole Generation... CULT®'.

posts.caption:
In the 2000s, Dior advertising was defined by sharp visuals and controlled sensuality under the direction of John Galliano, the creative director at the time. The campaigns combined high-gloss skin, strong poses, and precise styling.
Images ranged from intimate, sunlit shots with minimal denim looks and tinted sunglasses to more structured compositions with heavy textures, oversized frames, and rigid silhouettes. Softer elements like flowing gowns appeared, but were always presented with clarity and restraint.
The overall tone remained cool, polished, and deliberate.
posts.alt_text: Photo by CULT MAGAZINEⓇ on April 14, 2026. May be an image of magazine and text that says 'From the Archives: Dior Ad's in the 2000s... CULT'.

posts.caption:
Designers at work are often less about glamour than people expect. It’s quiet focus, scattered sketches, fabric samples, half-finished ideas.
There is something inherently inspiring about that space. A desk covered in references, a chair pulled in too close, a wall slowly turning into a process rather than a presentation.
It’s not about perfection in the moment, it’s about building it, piece by piece.

posts.caption:
The era of extremely oversized silhouettes and ultra-baggy jeans feels like slowly fading out. It defined a moment, loose fits, exaggerated proportions, everything deliberately big.
But style is always shifting.
Right now, there’s something more compelling about cleaner, closer fits. Clothes that follow the body a bit more naturally.
That doesn’t mean baggy jeans or oversized pieces are no longer relevant, they can still look great when styled well. It’s just a change in direction.
Personally, I prefer more fitted clothing at the moment.

posts.caption:
Pets, especially dogs and cats, are often seen today not only as companions but also as part of personal style and lifestyle aesthetics. In fashion and social media culture, they can appear like the perfect accessory, matching outfits, curated photos, and a sense of elevated lifestyle. In many cases, this can look beautiful and expressive, showing the bond between humans and animals in a visually appealing way.
At the same time, there is another side to this trend. Pets are sometimes acquired or used more for appearance than for responsibility or care. Instead of being fully respected as living beings with needs, they can unintentionally become part of a visual concept or “fashion statement.” This raises the question of whether they are always being treated in a truly appropriate and animal-friendly way.
Personally, I really like dogs and cats.
I believe they should always be treated with care and respect. The idea of them as a “fashion accessory” can be creative and aesthetic on one hand, but it should never come before their well-being and their role as loyal, living friends.

posts.caption:
June 12 and the album title says everything.
@oliviarodrigo just confirmed her third album You Seem Pretty Sad arrives June 12 and the details paint a picture of an artist who refuses to play it safe. The title alone is a statement. No pretense of happiness. No radio friendly polish on the emotional core.
After SOUR defined heartbreak for a generation and GUTS proved she could evolve beyond the debut formula this third album carries the weight of expectations that have buried artists with less backbone. Rodrigo seems unbothered by that pressure which is probably why the music works.
At 23 she has already built a catalog that spans pop punk ballad and alternative with enough authenticity to keep critics and fans aligned. The June release window puts her in direct competition with summer blockbuster albums which is a confidence play that says something about her team's belief in the material.
What vibe are you hoping for on this album: more SOUR or more GUTS?

⁠

But on social media, there is a growing shift in emphasis towards angular jawlines, visible cheekbones and “snatched waists” (a term which, incredibly, many straight men are now using in earnest).⁠ Now, it seems the goal is not to get as big and strong as possible, but to have as little body fat as possible.⁠

Responding to criticisms about the ways he portrays sex in 2023, Euphoria creator Sam Levinson said, “We live in a very sexualised world. Especially in the States, the influence of pornography is strong in the psyche of young people. We see this in pop music.” ⁠
⁠
But his work doesn’t critique this; it reflects it, and revels in it writes Jennie Jane in a new opinion column for Dazed.⁠
⁠
'I’m not sure if Levinson is aware of his own desire to see women so abjectly degraded in his work – but for us viewers, it’s abundantly clear. The whole thing is gross – as hard to stomach as those lubed-up fentanyl balls.'⁠

posts.caption:
There are multiple reasons why @wolkmindvirus2 could be scared of the internet. ⁠
⁠
When she was just 16, she posted a public suicide note, and it became a meme. Also, she’s known to divide a comments section. But to her fans, Wolk’s unfiltered style of comedy scratches an itch best suited for the digital age.⁠
⁠
Maybe you’ve seen her in Anora, or If I Had Legs I’d Kick You, but didn’t put it together that it was the same girl on screen that was posting every thought on her Instagram stories. ⁠
⁠
The 21-year-old does it all – and she’s committed not to abandon the internet that influenced her comedic sensibilities. ⁠

posts.caption:
#TheNewDiamondOrder 💎
The diamond industry isn’t being disrupted.
It’s being restructured.
Lab-grown diamonds have changed the game:
Pricing is no longer anchored in scarcity
Supply is no longer limited by geology
Consumers are no longer buying the same story
And the biggest shift?
👉 Diamonds are now competing with… diamonds.
This has created a clear divide:
✨ Natural = legacy, rarity, status
✨ Lab-grown = accessibility, size, smart value
For retailers and brands, this is massive:
Lab-grown = higher agility & better margins
Natural = stronger storytelling & luxury positioning
Meanwhile, Gen Z is rewriting the rulebook:
They don’t just ask “how it looks”
They ask “what it stands for”
Which means:
👉 Transparency, traceability & ESG are no longer optional.
The real insight?
This isn’t a product war.
It’s a positioning war.
#DiamondWorld #DWMagazine #NaturalDiamonds #LabGrownDiamonds

posts.caption:
The global diamond trade is entering a new phase — and India is right at the center of it.
With the EU–India FTA unlocking zero-duty access to one of the world’s largest jewellery markets, and the US tariff reset bringing partial relief, exporters are navigating both opportunity and uncertainty at the same time.
But here’s the real shift:
This is no longer a volume game.
Margins, compliance, storytelling, and agility are becoming the new competitive edge.
What this means for the industry:
• Europe is opening doors — but with stricter rules
• The US remains critical — but unpredictable
• Value-led diamonds are gaining importance
• Market diversification is no longer optional
The next growth phase will belong to businesses that can balance scale with strategy.
Save this if you’re planning your next export move.
#DiamondWorld #DWMagazine #DiamondIndustry #GJEPC #GlobalTrade
posts.alt_text: Photo by Diamond World on April 09, 2026. May be a cartoon of poster, magazine and text that says 'DW DAMONDWORLAD EU-India FTA EU-IndiaFTA&US FTA&US & US Tariff Pact A Turning Point for the Diamond Industry'.

This World Diamond Day, we celebrate the mine-to-market journey — where natural diamonds evolve into symbols of value, rarity, and global trade.
Because behind every polished diamond lies an industry built on craft, science, and trust.

posts.caption:
Lululemon, the workout brand that is famous for its rampant racism, controversial see-through leggings, and murder case that instantly became a true crime classic, is now under investigation in Texas for potentially using “forever chemicals” in its apparel. The investigation was opened by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton on Monday.
“I will not allow any corporation to sell harmful, toxic materials to consumers at a premium price under the guise of wellness and sustainability,” reads Paxton’s press release.
This isn’t the first time Lululemon has faced such allegations; Polyfluoroalkyls, commonly known as PFA’s, have been found in Lululemon products before. However, the company claims that they “phased out the substance” in 2023.
PFA’s are referred to as “forever chemicals” due to their extreme resistance to degradation, remaining intact in the environment for thousands of years. According to the EPA, we don’t yet know exactly how harmful they are to the human body, but they have been linked to reduced fertility and cancer and are considered highly toxic.
And then there is Ken Paxton who can also be considered highly toxic. He is a MAGA darling who has opened investigations on Tylenol for failing to warn pregnant women that the drug could cause autism in unborn children, and on parents who sought out gender-affirming care for their transgender children. He has been marred by scandals throughout his career, and he remains loyal to the Trump administration and to RFK’s MAHA initiatives. His press release concerning Lululemon is vague about why this investigation is being launched now, citing only “emerging research and consumer concerns.”
What is clear, here, is that both entities are working to send the message that they care about the wellness of their customers/constituents even though their track records tell a different tale. Lululemon and Paxton are each toxic in their own respect, each deserving their downfalls in equal measure. So, in a weird way, we’re happy they found each other, and we look forward to watching how their feud plays out.
#lululemon #kenpaxton #wellness

posts.caption:
@Coach is making a case for the WNBA that fashion’s next real front row is courtside seats. At the 2026 WNBA Draft in New York, the brand, who is the official handbag partner of the league and sponsors its Orange Carpet, dressed incoming players in custom looks designed by Stuart Vevers.
Azzi Fudd, Lauren Betts, Kiki Rice, Gabriela Jaquez, and Raven Johnson were each outfitted in pieces inspired by the codes of the brand’s Fall 2026 collection, like film noir romance and deconstructed tailoring, that were reworked through a collaborative process to reflect each athlete’s personal style.
The result lands somewhere between traditional draft-night polish and something more directional. Repurposed materials give subtle nods to ‘90s grunge and 1940s glamour, like slip dresses with slits, leather with “loveworn” finish, and tailored pieces with inside-out construction details.
The timing isn’t incidental. The WNBA has seen a surge in visibility in recent seasons, with rising attendance, record viewership, and a new generation of players drawing attention well beyond the court. As that audience grows, so does the visibility of the space around it, the arrivals, tunnel walks, and now the Orange Carpet.
For brands, sports are no longer just a sponsorship category but an increasingly viable runway: a place where personal style, performance, and cultural relevance converge in real time, and Coach is wise to have built their multi-year partnership with the league when they did.
#CoachPartner #coach #fashion #WNBA

posts.caption:
Looks like Pope Leo XIV is the target of Trump’s latest crashout per his Truth Social account, which includes an AI-generated image of himself seemingly as Jesus Christ.
Trump’s meltdown seems to trace back to the US-born pope’s criticism of the war against Iran, and more specifically the president’s statement last week threatening to end Iran’s “whole civilization.”
“Leo should get his act together as Pope, use Common Sense, stop catering to the Radical Left, and focus on being a Great Pope, not a Politician,” Trump posted, adding Pope Leo is also “weak on crime” (???)
Over the weekend, Pope Leo held a prayer vigil for peace and spoke about the “delusion of omnipotence that is becoming increasingly unpredictable and aggressive,” without naming the president or any other leaders.
When reporters pressed him Monday about Trump’s messaging, Pope Leo stated he was “not afraid of the Trump administration or of speaking out loudly about the message of the Gospel, which is what the Church works for.”
Trump pulled 55 percent of the Catholic vote in the 2026 election, per Pew Research Center, expanding his base from previous runs.
At this point, does he even have a line left to cross for his base?​​
#wtf #news #threads
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Diet Prada ™ on April 13, 2026 tagging @deanwithrs. May be a Twitter screenshot of text that says 'deanwithrs They called us crazy for saying Trump thinks he's King. As it turns out, he thinks he's God. Donald Trump @realDonaldTrump Trump'.

posts.caption:
On Thursday, Melania Trump held a rare conference to officially distance herself from Jeffrey Epstein and kill the story once and for all. Spoiler: she did not.
“The lies linking me with the disgraceful Jeffrey Epstein need to end today,” she declared. According to Melania, they were never friends, just occasional run-ins in NYC/Florida social circles. Nbd!
“I am not Epstein’s victim. Epstein did not introduce me to Donald Trump,” she said during the brief speech which lasted less than six minutes.
This marks the first time Melania has publicly acknowledged her connection to Epstein as First Lady, despite the well-documented archive of photos, emails, and receipts floating around.
The unexpected announcement dropped right as the Trump administration is already getting dragged by its own base over involvement with both Iran and the Epstein files.
After her remarks, the president told MSNOW in an interview that he didn’t “know anything about” what FLOTUS planned to say before she appeared on camera.
So why now? Why would someone as intentionally low-key as Melania choose this moment to enter the chat? 🤔
#reels #threads #receipts #viral #fyp

posts.caption:
Split-toe tabi shoes, specifically those from Maison Margiela, have long been a calling card for fashion folk, signalling the fashion literacy of their wearers quicker than perhaps any other fashion accessory. Tabis are to the downtown fashion insider what Birkins are to the uptown stylista, and they’ve held on to this slippery status for an inordinate amount of time.
But now, conspicuous dupes are popping up everywhere. Could the tabi be losing its insider appeal?
Martin Margiela, referencing Japanese footwear that dates back as far as the 15th century, first showed the tabi on his label’s debut runway in ‘88. The tabi quickly became a 90s icon for those in the know, with downtown it-girl Chloë Sevigny as an early adopter. Later, in ‘96, Nike released the Air Rift, a sleek sneaker with a Tabi-style split toe. Jennifer Aniston was photographed wearing the Air Rift with streetwear outfits, but for the most part the style remained confounding to the masses, which is likely why it became such a wink-and-nod classic for real fashion devotees.
As the 00’s embraced stilettos, skate shoes, and Ugg boots, the Tabi went dark for a while. That is, until the 2010s when the Olsen twins grew up and made 90s deconstructed minimalism hot again. Suddenly, the goalpost for fashionability shifted; The high-heeled supermodel look became gauche, replaced by baggy coats, unkempt hair, and footwear that subverted conventional beauty standards. The tabi found its way back onto the feet of fashion insiders lickety-split. And, just like in ‘88, the shoe was a bold signifier. The broader public took notice; Nike re-released the Air Rift in ‘15, and Vetements showed intentionally Margiela-inspired Tabis on their FW18 runway.
Now, you can grab a $50 “tabi” on Amazon. Or, Steve Madden and Jeffrey Campbell offer near exact replicas to Margiela’s. Kim K even teamed with Nike to slap a Skims logo on the Air Rift. No in-the-know fashion accessory could possibly survive such a fate, or does the tabi have what it takes to become an everyday silhouette?
#fashion #tabi
posts.alt_text: Photo by Diet Prada ™ on April 09, 2026. May be an image of boots, high-heeled shoes and text that says 'Fast Fashion Is Coming for Margiela's Tabi The style that once signaled insider status is now on shelves everywhere. Is it destined for mass , or done for? あメ Diet PR PRADA'.

posts.caption:
You know how a lot of collections look great on the runway but just don’t translate into real personal style, don’t feel wearable, don’t feel desirable beyond the show, this is the opposite of that, and more and more I’m noticing younger designers pushing craft into a space that feels genuinely international, not “global” as a buzzword but actually wearable across countries and contexts, nothing feels stale, it actually feels current, and we use the word modern a lot but this really feels like it, it reads like ready-to-wear but a more evolved version of it, it doesn’t feel like something you need to carry or protect, you can just throw it on with jeans and step out, it’s light, wearable, easy in the best way, and this brand has completely nailed that.
The brand is @cdk_gyencha, CDK standing for ChanDriKa and GYENCHA meaning ornament, and while the story is strong, what really hits is how it shows up in the clothes, because this return to Bhutan after two years doesn’t just stay sentimental, it feels like a real reconnection with people, place, and memory without getting heavy, and you can see that even in the casting. The show is styled by @dhfranklin, who works with the brand to bring Bhutanese handweaving into a contemporary, minimal, slightly experimental space that honours the roots without freezing them, making it feel current and, most importantly, desirable.

posts.caption: The SK-II launch in India means a few things. First, it is the end of constant outsourcing. No more begging NRI cousins or paying heavy shipping charges just for a restock. Second, their OG trade secret, the fermented PITERA™️ formula will finally be in the Indian beauty cabinets (some of you are ahead of the market game and are already in your 9th or 10th year of using it!!). This Japanese brand has had a genuine chokehold on the skincare community way before Instagram could generate a new product of the month! The science girlies even have an actual study to validate its claims. After much anticipation SK-II finally makes its India debut and is sold on Nykaa!

posts.caption:
🚨New series alert 🚨 The A-Z of INDIAN DESIGNERS you need to know in 2026!!
(B for) Baroodi is one of designer Aiman Verma’s two brands. That is, brands founded and run by younger designers who tap into heritage and craft without polluting it with modern commercial buzz.
Baroodi is structured out of more traditional forms of living. Techniques and centuries-old craft get revisited without any dilution of its original regality and elegance. A nod to nostalgic good old days and sepia-toned fantasies. Less historical accuracy and more vibes. A “pure indulgence”. His collections run on the 808s of dualities— traditional paisleys are framed in American workwear, and pashmina shawls trade bodies with luxury jackets. A wonderland where any and everything is an inspiration, and (like for his last collection) objects like buttons form an unassuming muse. Every piece is slowly and consistently made to be contemporary heirlooms.
The reflection of his Gemini (moon), he likes to see his two brands as his split personalities, and Baroodi is the grounded one in that equation. Verma, in his own words (and voice), tells us what experiences and philosophies help him create and aid in finding a place for Baroodi in an explosive market like contemporary fashion.
posts.alt_text: Photo by DietSabya® on April 11, 2026. May be an image of bolo tie and text.

posts.caption:
Since y’all asked for it. Diamonds have always been ✨coded✨ as occasion-markers. The pear-shaped for the first-job flex, the princess-cut for a promotion-era glow-up, the biggest one for a divorce reset why lie. A default purchase for life’s so-called “milestone-moments”?
And what about the heirloom-heavy, memory-loaded stash we all love to romanticise? On World Diamond Day, I want the girlies, gaylies, and theylies to tell me their reasoning behind buying real diamonds. What actually makes it special? And why? Are we leaning into heirloom-meets-new, stack-it-back styling? Or are we building fresh-from-scratch, self-purchase collections entirely?
So many questions. Let’s commence Diamond-Index 2026 👀☕️💎

posts.caption:
#ELLECoverStar: There are artists who arrive with noise, and then there are artists like Zayn Malik (@zayn), the kind who never really needed to raise his voice to command attention. Some people have presence; Malik has always had pull. Fifteen years after his debut, and nearly a decade into his solo career, Malik returns to ELLE India, where he first made history in 2018 with his first-ever Indian magazine cover. There’s no forced “full circle” narrative here, no overworked nostalgia. If anything, this moment feels less like a return and more like a continuation — the same artist, only more settled into himself. “I don’t feel like I’m at such a different place in my career,” he says. “I feel like I’m at a different place and point as a human; I’m developing, growing, and learning from life experiences.” That distinction feels important. The artistry was never in question. If anything, it is the life around it that now feels more fully aligned. Head to the 🔗in bio to read the full conversation with our cover star.

posts.caption:
#ELLECulture: Gen Z is rewriting the rules of networking — ditching cold emails and LinkedIn formalities for something far more direct (and dare we say, effective). Dating apps are no longer just about romance. They’re becoming unexpected spaces for opportunity, where a match can just as easily lead to a job referral as it can to a situationship.
Because when traditional applications disappear into algorithms, conversation becomes currency — and Gen Z knows exactly where to find it.
Read more at the 🔗 in bio.
Words by: @taronish.batty￼

posts.caption:
#ELLEFashion: Zara Larsson (@zaralarsson) has been an absolute vision and we are NOT taking the whimsy for granted 🧚
From music videos to the ‘Midnight Sun’ tour, every look has been a maximalist moment. The outfits, the glam, the whole world she’s built around this era? Pop Maximalism is back and we know exactly who brought it.
All credit to Sophia Sinot (@sophiasinot) for the extraterrestrial siren makeup — gems, body glitter, sorbet lids, Y2K nostalgia — and to the styling that matched every single second of it. Together they created something that felt less like a tour and more like a fashion editorial that just happened to be set on a stage. Girl said fun is back. And we felt that🦋✨

posts.caption:
#ELLEFashion: Around the premieres of The Devil Wears Prada, Meryl Streep delivered a red carpet wardrobe that felt like a seamless extension of Miranda Priestly—anchored in sharp tailoring and quiet authority. From that commanding Yves Saint Laurent military jacket to sleek black dresses, softly draped evening gowns, and polished separates from houses like Prada and Donna Karan, her looks stayed rooted in structure and a sort of command. The palette rarely strayed far from blacks, the iconic red, and deep tones, letting presence take the lead—each appearance feeling deliberate, composed, and unmistakably powerful without ever tipping into excess.
Meryl Streep

posts.caption:
@oliviadeano is everywhere—and for good reason.
Since the September release of her hit sophomore album, ‘The Art of Loving,’ the British songstress has been riding a serious wave of momentum on both sides of the pond. Add in a Grammy win, standout live performances, a @cartier campaign, and critical raves, and it’s clear: Dean’s starpower is undeniable. As she prepares to kick off the album’s sold-out world tour, Dean is trying her best to soak it all in—and define success on her own terms.
In ELLE’s Women in Music cover story, @sylviaobell chats with the Grammy winner about navigating public opinion in her newfound spotlight, the wisdom the women in her life have imparted on her, and the precedent she hopes to set with her upcoming tour: “I don’t think you need to be someone who’s got loads of money to enjoy your favorite album.” Read the full interview at the link in bio.

Years into a formidable career, Charan isn’t chasing expectation… he’s refining it. Choosing presence over performance and meaning over mere spectacle.
We celebrate his journey with our latest cover, where the actor steps into a version of himself like never seen before.

posts.caption:
Ram Charan (@alwaysramcharan) found that what began as discipline slowly took on deeper meaning. The Ayyappa Deeksha—observed by his father over twenty-eight times and by Charan himself nearly fifteen—now serves as a quiet reset in a life defined by constant motion and visibility. “It gives me a complete reset,” he says. “It gives me grounding. It gives me stability in my mental thoughts completely. And it helps me not get attracted to any vices or temptations in life.”

posts.caption:
This is the best thing found on the Internet in a long time. Obsessed.
Now I’m trying to take it all to heart as I peruse my next collection
posts.alt_text: Photo by Fashion is Psychology™️ on March 19, 2025.

posts.caption:
“Hi Shakaila we’re xyz producers and we’d love to make a tv show with you” I’ve had a handful of those conversations over the years and they quickly go from 🤩 to 🫩
I used to feel disheartened until I looked around and saw so many examples of people not waiting for permission and instead deciding to get up and make shit happen! So that’s what I decided to do, turn this decades long dream into a reality! I pulled in every favour I had! Turned my friends into producers and show runners, begged and borrowed and you know what, it turned out pretty damn good if I do say so myself 😅
This black history month especially, I’m inspired by the talented Black British women who have shown me that it’s possible to make a mark on the small screen no matter the limitations!
I absolutely loved makeover shows growing up! But as I got older I saw how they were more brutal than beautiful!
Today, fashion psychology shows us just how deeply our clothes impact our mental health, identity, and confidence.
That’s why I’m so excited to share The Wearapy Show my new makeover series that blends fashion psychology, emotional healing and real style transformations.
We help everyday people reclaim their wardrobes after major life shifts: grief, motherhood, identity struggles, and more.
With a team of experts and no judgment, just science-backed guidance and love.
Episodes 1 and 2 launch 26th October on the Fashion is Psychology YouTube Channel. Subscribe now and follow the journey.
#bhm #wearapy #fashionpsychology #makeover

posts.caption:
HUGE NEWS: Good On You has officially rated over 7,000 brands 🎉 This is a major accomplishment, as even more brands—from the small and indie through the large and luxury—are in our Directory.⁠
⁠
What goes into ratings? 🤔 A lot, actually: we have a rigorous, comprehensive system analysing up to 1,000 data points across 100 key issues to arrive at our lowest score (“We Avoid”) to our highest score (“Great”). Ratings are based solely on a brand’s transparent, public reporting that we pair with credible third-party indices, certifications, and standards.⁠
⁠
How do we select brands to rate? Well…with your help 🥹 Many of our ratings come through community requests, shared in comments and messages, which helps us understand which brands to prioritise to meet this moment. Additionally, our retail and ecommerce partners along with brands request ratings too, which is a key means to apply change to the industry from within.⁠
⁠
Another item to celebrate here? Of these rated brands, 1,000 have received our two highest scores, “Good” and “Great”. This is very exciting news, representative of how more brands are doing considering their impact on workers, the planet, and animals. (Even if that also means…6000 brands have a lot more to do to reduce their impact.)⁠
⁠
Here’s to continuing to keep people like you informed while supporting more sustainable brands that are making the difference! Cheers 🥂 Explore our 7,000 ratings at the link too ✨⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #SustainableStyle #ConsciousStyle #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion⁠
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on January 21, 2026. May be an image of poster.

posts.caption:
Meet the top rated brands of 2025 🎊⁠
⁠
In the past year, we rated more than 1500 brands and are proud to share the ten that received our highest marks, illustrating a commitment to more sustainable practices that protect people, the planet, and animals 💚⁠
⁠
What’s a theme that unites these brands? 👀 Outside of a commitment to reducing their impact, these brands are all small and independent, a reminder of where to look in the industry for leadership and innovation. If you’re looking for a reminder to support small brands this year, this is it.⁠
⁠
Cheers to all these brands, along with all the brands that were rated “Good” and “Great” in the past year! Here’s to continuing to make a difference and for championing a better approach to what we wear. Find the full list at the link—and share which brands you’re hoping to see rated in 2026 in the comments 💬⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #SustainableBeauty #EcoBeauty #Beauty #BeautyBrand #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on January 08, 2026.

posts.caption:
What can be accomplished in ten years?⁠
⁠
A decade may seem short but, in reflecting on all the work we’ve done in the past decade, we’re in awe of everything that has transpired—and are grateful for the hard work of the current and former team members along with you.⁠
⁠
Whether you’ve shared our posts or have been a partner in this business, the work of creating a more sustainable future isn’t done alone but in partnership with others. These accomplishments are an example of what happens when likeminded people come together in the hopes of a better world 🌍🌎🌏⁠
⁠
Swipe to learn about ten things Good On You has accomplished in the past ten years. Share how you think the sustainable fashion and beauty industry have shifted in the past decade 💚⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #SustainableBeauty #EcoBeauty #Beauty #BeautyBrand #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on September 03, 2025.

posts.caption:
Good news and bad news 😬⁠
⁠
The good? Of the 362 brands we’ve rated so far this year, 28—or 8%—scored our highest ratings of “Good” and “Great”. These brands represent leadership in sustainable fashion and beauty, and should definitely be on your list of brands to watch 💚⁠
⁠
The bad? Our analysts found that a third of brands rated (113!) rated our lowest score, “We Avoid”. These are brands working non-transparently, who are notable for their doing the bare minimum, if anything at all 🫨⁠
⁠
Swipe to learn who these brands are ⏩ Which brands do you want to have rated next? Share in the comments, then explore the full story at the link →↑⁠
⁠
#Shopping #SustainableFashion #Fashion #OOTD #StyleInspiration

posts.caption:
On? Hoka? Salomon? Are any of these brands sustainable? 👀⁠
⁠
This is a complicated question, as the brands rate within a few points of each other, with one of them falling behind. Can you guess which one?⁠
⁠
However, none of them are really winning the race of reducing impact on people, the planet, and animals: all of them score much lower than we prefer, signalling how the sneakers and athletic wear continues to lack a sustainable savvy 🫠⁠
⁠

The go-to star for prestige British drama, if a TV show has Keeley Hawes in it, you know it’s going to be good.
With a CV that reads like a list of the nation’s favourite shows - Spooks, Line of Duty, Ashes to Ashes, Bodyguard, The Durrells, It’s A Sin, to name a handful - next up @misskeeleyhawes plays a nun in Channel 4 drama Falling, from Adolescence writer Jack Thorne.
In the new issue of Grazia she talks to @lauracraik about confidence, telling women’s stories and the not-so glamorous part of her job.
TAP the link in bio to read now.

posts.caption:
Jewellery that sings and moves with you? Clash de Cartier is just that and then some. First introduced in 2019, the latest additions to the collection, defined by their architectural forms, bring colour and movement, making them among the most versatile (and hardest working) creations around, and exactly what we want to wear with our favourite wardrobe staples.
Stack them, go bold or pare it back. Clash de Cartier already feels like a modern classic and an icon in the making.
Pictured from top: Clash de Cartier ring, 18K rose gold, red-dyed agate, £5,400; Clash de Cartier ring 18K rose gold, green-dyed agate, £5,400; Clash de Cartier ring 18K rose gold, pink chalcedony, £5,400; Clash de Cartier ring 18K rose gold, onyx, £4,700.
Clash de Cartier necklace, double row model, 18K yellow gold, £23,500

Styling: Molly Haylor

From reprising her role in Shrek 5 to filming ‘Sham’, a rom-com alongside Stephen Merchant, before a quick detour to the red carpet to promote her latest movie ‘Outcome’ opposite Keanu Reeves. Rather than making a low-key return to acting, Diaz is diving straight back in, and we are here for it.

@AnneHathaway is the cover star of Bazaar’s April Now Issue—our annual look at the artists and creators shaping the culture. And indeed, it may soon feel like she’s everywhere, given that she’s about to star in three films: reprising her most millennial role as Andy Sachs in “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” taking on pop stardom in “Mother Mary,” and playing Penelope in Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey.” At the link in our bio, she opens up about her role in the culture and the way she’s seen it change over her two-decade career.

posts.caption:
It’s been 20 years since @AnneHathaway starred in “The Devil Wears Prada” as the plucky Andie Sachs, an aspiring journalist who takes a job at “Runway” magazine assisting editor in chief Miranda Priestly (played meme-ably by Meryl Streep). The movie made her the patron saint of earnest millennial ambition. Now, Hathaway returns to Andy Sachs at a time when both she and the culture have outgrown the fantasy of having it all. At the link in bio, Hathaway opens up about her career, aging, and finding her own way forward in our April cover story.
-

posts.caption: At Tiffany & Co., good things come in little blue boxes, especially those that come from Blue Books. At the link in bio, Senior Vice President and Chief Artistic Officer of High Jewelry and Jewelry Nathalie Verdeille shares the inspiration behind intricate treasures and walks us through the latest collection. posts.caption:
@gap turned Coachella into a hoodie playground, and everyone showed up.
Amid the festival’s sea of brand activations, the electric-blue Hoodie House stood out like a mirage of cool, drawing the longest lines all weekend. Inside, fans customized GAP’s iconic hoodies with Coachella-branded logos, patches, metallic charms, and bead details, all heat-pressed into one-of-a-kind keepsakes. The space doubled as a cultural time capsule, decked out with oversized records from past GAP collaborators like Tyla, N.E.R.D, and Madonna, plus Polaroid moments and claw machine goodies to seal the experience.
Beyond the aesthetics, the magic lived in the stories. Festival-goers spoke about comfort, nostalgia, and even post-breakup reinvention through their hoodies, while others leaned into oversized fits inspired by Bieber-core dreams. Whether tied to dance class memories, shared wardrobes, or childhood shopping sprees, the throughline was clear: GAP isn’t just clothing, it’s emotional uniform. And at Coachella, it proved once again that hoodie season never really ends.
✍ @ericacxmpbell

posts.caption:
This past weekend, Highsnobiety’s visuals editor @azraschorr stopped by the buzzy @motherdaughterholyspirit.nyc closet sale with a mission– to get a cool piece from a cool person. The sale featured items from donors like @pascalispunk , @juliafox , and @lenawaithe , with all the sale’s proceeds going to the Trans Justice Funding Project.
At the Friday preview, an MDHS co-organizer named Bobbi walked Highsnobiety through a few favorite pieces; @richieshazam ’s Puppets and Puppets dress, a Spring 25’ Rick Owens Hooded Stooges Jacket, a Miss Claire Sullivan Poof Skirt with matching Sash and Bodysuit.
It was, really, the perfect way to spend a New York weekend: Where else could the city’s most fashionable have a chance to shop Chloë Sevigny’s Palace x Vivienne Westwood tracksuit with a matching devil-horn hat AND support a wonderful cause?
The sale went on over the weekend, but if you missed it, some pieces are still available to shop on their website.
Images by: @azraschorr

posts.caption:
@_sagenation pants have always made a scene, now its shoes are stepping in to match.⁠
⁠
Since 2020, Sage Toda-Nation’s London label has built its identity on exaggerated, sculptural trousers, and now it’s expanding into footwear with the Gemstone 001. Handmade in Italy with Officine Creative, the shoe blends dress and utility cues, from sleek buffalo leather and a heeled sole to hiking-style lace hooks and a workwear-inspired toe cap. Designed around an ergonomic “foot-like” last, it mirrors the brand’s balance of refinement and irregularity, pairing naturally with its oversized silhouettes. ⁠
⁠
With prior success in reworking classic footwear at YMC, Toda-Nation now pushes further into experimental territory, marking the next phase of Sage Nation’s growing wardrobe ecosystem.
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 15, 2026 tagging @highsnobietysneakers. May be an image of wingtip shoes, sweatpants, sneakers, toecap shoes and text that says 'HE DESIGNED BIG SHOES TO MATCH HIS HUGE PANTS (EXCLUSIVE) H'.

posts.caption:
Rain, frozen mid-fall, quite poetic. ⁠
⁠
@pharrell turned a fleeting moment into fashion for @louisvuitton Fall/Winter 2026, designing garments that look permanently soaked yet remain completely dry. Inspired by the oddly satisfying water-beading effect seen on performance jackets like Arc’teryx, LV pieces feature intricate glass bead appliqués that mimic raindrops clinging to fabric. From mac coats to technical shells, the illusion is strikingly real, with droplets appearing denser at the top and fading downward, as if gravity were at work.⁠
⁠
This subtle trompe l’oeil detail, easily missed during a runway show dominated by a full-scale house set — extends across outerwear, shirts, and even sneakers. Like other high-fashion illusions that trick the eye into seeing something that isn’t there, LV’s “wet look” captures a moment of rainfall and suspends it in time, blending performance aesthetics with couture-level craftsmanship.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 15, 2026. May be an image of duffle coat, overcoat, suit, turtleneck and text that says 'O EVERYONE MISSED THIS DRIPPY LOUIS VUITTON DESIGN TRICK Η'.

posts.caption:
@universalgeneve isn’t wasting any time. The Swiss watchmaker was founded in 1894 but has spent almost three decades out of the game, a quartz crisis-fueled sabbatical that has meant it’s best known today for being prized by vintage collectors. ⁠
⁠
Now, it's returning with a vengeance. ⁠
⁠
At Watches & Wonders, the biggest annual event in horology, Universal Genève relaunched with an expansive offering: 36 new watches split between five models fitted with three new movements. ⁠
⁠
Highsnobiety’s eyes and ears on the ground at Watches & Wonders, @scarlintheshire, got an IRL look at all the company’s new creations and even tried on some of the watches. Swipe for a closer look.⁠
⁠
📷️: Scarlett Baker
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 14, 2026 tagging @universalgeneve. May be an image of wrist watch and text.

posts.caption:
@willychavarria is teaming back up with @adidasoriginals SS26, this time around tying in a "Love Prevails" theme.⁠
⁠
The duo's SS26 collection spans sneakers and apparel, but nothing about it feels standard. From sharply constructed tailoring to elevated footwear, the collection pushes beyond familiar sportswear codes into something more refined. Chavarria took on the adidas Superstar for the footwear offering, dishing out two colorways with a special rose-toe design instead of the classic shell toe.⁠
⁠
In typical Willy Chavarria fashion, the "Love Prevails" collection is more than clothes; it's a social forward statement tied back to community, resilience, and love. The collaborative capsule is slated to be dropped on April 18 via adidas and select retailers.

posts.caption:
@moncler brings its puffer DNA to a summer wardrobe.
Designed for the seasonal weather shift, classics are reimagined for the summer months as the brand is bringing its signature puffer tech to lightweight layers and refined silhouettes. Fabrics like nylon shell are paired with floral-quilted shirt jackets while outer layers are enriched with intricate details including bow closures and cinchable, lace-up waists.
For its Puffy Summer campaign, Moncler has enlisted set designer @andyhillmanstudio to create a series of inflatable animal sculptures inspired by the collection
Puffy Summer installations will also be taking over pop-ups and Moncler flagships around the world this year, kick starting at Milan Design Week until April 28.
In partnership with Moncler

posts.caption:
If you’re into ‘The Sims’ at all, you’ve probably stumbled across a @kokoshkapino video before. Creating heartwarming, nostalgic slices of life and suspenseful thrillers that will have you hanging on to the edge of your phone screen, the multidisciplinary artist has made the game her canvas.
Making Academy Award-worthy cinema using an early 2000s video game is a feat in and of itself, but look at the comments of any of her videos and you’ll see why people love them so much. “My favorite show is on” is a standard reply to her creations, with many storylines blending into each other and leaving her audience wanting more.
We talked to Anastasia Kokoshka about her relationship with ‘The Sims,’ the process behind her viral videos and the potential for a career in film down the line. Head to the link in bio for the full conversation.
Images: Anastasia Kokoshka

posts.caption:
In a cinematic landscape that often equates love with grand gestures and emotional upheaval, restraint can feel almost subversive.
Imran Khan (@imrankhan) built his romances in the in-between moments, in pauses held a second longer, in conversations that lingered, in the quiet certainty of simply showing up. His love stories were never engineered for spectacle or soaring declarations; they unfolded with emotional intelligence, anchored in reassurance rather than chaos.
At a time when intensity is frequently mistaken for depth, his characters offered something rarer, steadiness. They made vulnerability feel unguarded yet assured, tender yet self-possessed.
This Valentine’s, we honour that softer grammar of romance. We honour The Minimalists.
Intention over excess.
Depth over drama.
Clarity over chaos.
A return to love that feels grounded, deliberate, and quietly profound.

posts.caption:
After over 10 years in the making, the doors to the @vam_east are almost ready to open to the public. Before that, though, the architects took us for a tour inside.
London's Stratford has seen unprecedented development and change over the past two decades. First came the Olympics in 2012, and now, building on that legacy, the area is becoming a huge cultural quarter for the city's Eastern boroughs.
A cornerstone of that is V&A East, designed by architects O’Donnell + Tuomey. With its facade being a nod to the area's past, a key part of their inspiration has come from ensuring future visitors know that the museum is somewhere designed for them, not against them.
From outside, you walk straight into the building (past Thomas J Price’s 18ft sculpture), with no front desk to block your way. Wayfinding by @fieldworkfacility makes different exhibitions easy to find, and along your route, there are places to perch and just spend time.
Inside, local practice @ja__projects has created the permanent exhibition space "Why We Make", in collaboration with @apracticeforeverydaylife, @larryachiampong and the V&A East Youth Collective. With a huge emphasis on inclusivity and participation, the flexible layouts are inspired by markets and public gathering spaces.
If the success of the nearby V&A Storehouse is anything to go by, the V&A East is set to become a gem of the East Bank
Photography:  @huftonandcrow and David Parry
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Hypeform on April 16, 2026 tagging @hypebeast, and @hypebeastuk. May be an image of magazine, buildings and text that says 'Hypefom London's New V&A East is Designed to be Your Museum'.

posts.caption:
Step inside @nike and @jumpman23's new SoHo space.⁠
⁠
It’s temporary, and it won’t look the same every time you walk in. Set at 611 Broadway, the layout shifts with what’s happening in the city, from football to the US Open to marathon season.⁠
⁠
Inside keeps it very New York with graffiti touches and industrial finishes. You’ve got NikeSKIMS, Nike By You with SoHo-only options by LAAMS, plus a Jersey By You station. Jordan Brand shows up too with a Brazil kit and the “Soul Tower” archive setup.⁠
⁠
Swipe for a closer look.⁠
Photo/Video: Brian T Nguyen/Hypebeast

posts.caption:
@sean_wotherspoon is taking his signature color-blocking to die-cast cars. ⁠
⁠
Teaming up with tarmac.works, the first drop centers on the Audi R8 LMS GT3, reworked through his lens and leading the rollout.⁠
⁠
The Nissan GT-R R32 follows as part two, set to land after the initial drop. Rounding it out is a full set of trading cards and a hoodie, tee, and cap to carry the collab beyond the display case.⁠
⁠
The Audi model lands April 17 to 19 at Beijing HEC, followed by an online release on May 12. The Nissan model will arrive at a later date, with details still to come.⁠
Photo: @jackwaterlotstudio

posts.caption:
@umathurman Chooses Happiness: For decades, Thurman has embodied a certain kind of unstoppable presence, and  this month, the icon returns to the grisly genre that cemented her stardom, as a bloodthirsty avenger in #PrettyLethal. But off-screen things are less grim: Thurman is energized by her work and looking forward to the possibilities of her next chapter—one where she’s making space for new roles, new interests, and more time for herself: “I think the choice to be happy is one that should be made regardless of any conditions.”
Read more now at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio.

posts.caption:
Man of the Moment — Yashasvi Jaiswal (@yashasvijaiswal28) continues to rise, and we’re here for every second of it.
From dreaming of centuries in blue to turning those visions into reality and now hitting it out of the park as the opener for Rajasthan Royal (@rajasthanroyals) in IPL, his journey is the very definition of hard work meeting opportunity. Powered as much by an unshakable mindset as by his brilliance on the pitch, our cover star opens up about the drive, discipline, and hunger that fuel his remarkable ascent, “I don’t know where I’m going to go, but I want to enjoy the journey.”
Starting out as a cricketing prodigy, now 24, he’s all about keeping his head in the game, stopping every now and then, to savour the simple joys of life. And maybe that is the secret sauce to success, one game at a time.
Here’s raising a toast to the ‘player of the match’- Yashasvi Jaiswal!

posts.caption:
Introducing L’OFFICIEL’s cover star, @penelopecruzoficial!
The actor found her calling at a young age, imagining herself in other worlds and pretending to be someone else. What began as a childhood game became a lifelong pursuit—not to escape, but to understand people and the world around her.
Cruz still brings that same curiosity and depth to her work today. Up next, she’ll star opposite Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale in Maggie Gyllenhaal’s ‘The Bride!’ Inspired by James Whale’s 1935 film ‘The Bride of Frankenstein,’ a bold gothic thriller casts Cruz as a detective hunting real-life monsters in the 1930s while also struggling for recognition in a male-dominated field. “She has to fight very hard,” Cruz tells L’OFFICIEL. “The same way women everywhere, in every profession, still have to work extremely hard to be valued.”
It’s a sentiment Cruz knows well. After decades of working non-stop—at one point making four films a year—the Oscar winner and longtime Chanel muse is now more selective with her roles. “I never regret the things that I say ‘No’ to,” she says. “Especially if the reason is just to be with my family and to raise my kids in a very present way.”
Visit the link in our bio for more about Cruz’s intentional approach to her work, her relationship with fame, her role in ‘The Bride!’, and more.

posts.caption:
Nuovo capitolo, stessa intensità. @penelopecruzoficial è la nostra nuova cover star e torna sullo schermo con The Bride!, diretto da Maggie Gyllenhaal.
Più di trent’anni di carriera, iniziata a 17 anni con Jamón Jamón di Bigas Luna, sette film con Pedro Almodóvar, un Oscar per Vicky Cristina Barcelona. Europa e Stati Uniti come due case, lo sguardo sempre acceso sul presente.
Nell’intervista a L’OFFICIEL, Penélope racconta l’incontro con Maggie e la nascita di un progetto che vibra di verità:
«Conosco Maggie da molti anni e abbiamo sempre desiderato lavorare insieme. Ho adorato il suo primo film, The Lost Daughter, tratto da un libro di Elena Ferrante. Lavorare con lei è stata una bellissima esperienza. Ha realizzato un film molto speciale, profondo, a tantissimi livelli. Visivamente attraente, molto rock and roll. C’è tanta verità».
Cinema che scava, immagini che restano. Penélope, come sempre, va dritta al cuore.

posts.caption:
@Gucci riscrive il suo heritage in seta con The Art of Silk, un progetto che porta dieci foulard d’archivio nel presente senza perdere un grammo di memoria. Selezionati da Demna, i motivi storici tornano con una nuova energia: più fluidi, più styling-driven, più now.
Tra Flora, animalia e codici nautici, il foulard non è più solo un dettaglio chic, ma un vero statement piece. E la parte più bella è che questo nuovo capitolo lega archivio, arte e savoir-faire italiano in un modo che feels both iconic and current.
Photo: @Gucci

posts.caption:
Introducing our cover star, Grace Burns!
The model steps into the spotlight in standout looks from the spring runways in Paris, where American sportswear’s influence was impossible to miss. But even as she follows in the footsteps of her mother, the legendary Christy Turlington Burns (@cturlington), @graciebrns wasn’t always set on a career in fashion.
“For as long as I can remember, I worried about when, if ever, I would find my passion or purpose, because that’s what I saw in my parents,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. Also a photographer, writer, and actor, she’s equally invested in building a creative life that feels expansive and honest, exploring different mediums while still discovering what feels most like her. Head to the link in our bio to hear more from Burns, and to see her in some of the season’s best American sportswear–inspired looks from across the Atlantic.

posts.caption:
Introducing L’OFFICIEL’s cover star, @penelopecruzoficial!
The actor found her calling at a young age, imagining herself in other worlds and pretending to be someone else. What began as a childhood game became a lifelong pursuit—not to escape, but to understand people and the world around her.
Cruz still brings that same curiosity and depth to her work today. Up next, she’ll star opposite Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale in Maggie Gyllenhaal’s ‘The Bride!’ Inspired by James Whale’s 1935 film ‘The Bride of Frankenstein,’ a bold gothic thriller casts Cruz as a detective hunting real-life monsters in the 1930s while also struggling for recognition in a male-dominated field. “She has to fight very hard,” Cruz tells L’OFFICIEL. “The same way women everywhere, in every profession, still have to work extremely hard to be valued.”
It’s a sentiment Cruz knows well. After decades of working non-stop—at one point making four films a year—the Oscar winner and longtime Chanel muse is now more selective with her roles. “I never regret the things that I say ‘No’ to,” she says. “Especially if the reason is just to be with my family and to raise my kids in a very present way.”
Visit the link in our bio for more about Cruz’s intentional approach to her work, her relationship with fame, her role in ‘The Bride!’, and more.

In the new issue of L’OFFICIEL USA, the cover star @falachenfala shared her unique understanding of love. Whether interpreting roles as an actress, creating stories as a director, or embodying different identities in life, she always believes that love is the fundamental driving force behind all actions. She observes the world with curiosity and responds to emotions with creativity. This approach to love enables her to stay clear and continuously progress at different stages of life. Now, she understands the importance of finding a balance between giving and receiving, expressing love in different ways, and letting love be a source of strength.
To learn more about Fala’s life story, stay tuned for the upcoming cover story!

posts.caption:
Something old, something new, something borrowed, something… red? As New York Bridal Fashion Week Spring/Summer 2027 has wrapped, designers made a compelling case for moving beyond white altogether.⁠
⁠
Across collections, color was paired with equally intentional details—sculptural silhouettes, sleek satin finishes, and tailoring that leaned almost ready-to-wear. Whether it came in the form of a sweeping ballgown or a pared-back column, the message was consistent: bridal doesn’t have to rely on tradition to feel significant.⁠
⁠
At the link in our bio, see which bridal trends are defining the season—and how designers are reshaping the idea of the modern bride.⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
Bridgerton isn’t just a romantic period drama, it’s one of Netflix’s most lavish productions. The series reportedly carries a staggering budget of around $168 million, placing it among the most expensive shows created by the streaming giant.
Each episode is estimated to cost around $7 million, funding the show’s extravagant costumes, grand sets, and cinematic storytelling that recreates Regency-era London. The scale of production is what gives the series its signature opulence and visual richness.
The spending doesn’t stop at sets and wardrobes. Reports also reveal impressive cast salaries, with some actors and voice talents earning up to $1 million for their roles, reflecting the show’s massive global success.
From lavish balls to royal romances, Bridgerton proves that luxury storytelling comes with a royal price tag and Netflix is willing to pay for it.
Images: Courtesy of Netflix
#Netflix #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Indian cricketer Jasprit Bumrah was recently seen wearing the ₹36-lakh @JacobAndCo Epic X ‘Sher-e-Punjab’ Edition, a distinctive timepiece that blends high-end watchmaking with strong cultural inspiration.
Created by Jacob & Co as a tribute to Sikh heritage, the watch features a lightweight 44mm titanium case and a skeletonised dial that reveals the intricate mechanics within. The vertical bridges are engraved with meaningful Sikh symbols, including the lion representing courage and leadership, the Khanda symbolising strength and unity, and the sacred Ek Onkar reflecting the belief in one universal God.
The titanium structure is paired with a bold blue honeycomb-textured rubber strap, a colour closely associated with Sikh identity. At its core is the hand-wound JCAM02 movement, offering a 48-hour power reserve while showcasing the watch’s detailed inner workings through the open dial.
Limited to just 50 pieces worldwide, the Sher-e-Punjab Edition honours the legacy of Guru Gobind Singh and the founding of the Khalsa, making it both a luxury watch and a tribute to heritage and resilience. ⌚✨

posts.caption:
A mysterious Aston Martin prototype has been spotted—and it’s unlike anything the brand has revealed before.
Resembling a fusion of a Formula 1 car and the Valkyrie AMR Pro, the extreme single-seater was captured during a private test, showcasing radical aerodynamics and a design built purely for speed. ￼
What makes it even more exclusive—it’s reportedly a one-off commission for billionaire Ken Griffin, developed by Aston Martin Performance Technologies with a bold ambition: to create the fastest track car in the world. ￼
Early reports suggest performance figures that could push boundaries beyond current Formula 1 cars, with whispers of lap times significantly quicker than today’s elite race machines. ￼
Built without the constraints of racing regulations, this one-of-one hypercar represents pure, unrestricted engineering—an extreme vision of what happens when limitless resources meet cutting-edge motorsport innovation.
#Luxurious

Range Rover celebrates 20 years of performance luxury with the new Sport Twenty Edition—a limited-run model that honors the legacy of one of its most iconic SUVs.
Finished in a striking Sanguinello Orange, this exclusive edition stands out with ‘TWENTY’ badging, 23-inch wheels, and a bold, unmistakable presence.
Inside, it features Ebony Windsor leather, forged carbon accents, and refined details that perfectly balance sportiness with signature Range Rover luxury.
Under the hood, powerful engine options—including a twin-turbo V8—deliver the performance to match its commanding design, staying true to the Sport’s high-performance DNA.
Pricing for the Range Rover Sport lineup starts at around $115,000 globally, while in India it ranges from approximately ₹1.38 crore up to ₹2.75 crore depending on the variant, making this anniversary edition a true collector’s statement. ￼
